heading: Terrorism prevention
label: 607 Terrorism prevention
notes: Amendments2016—Subsec. (a)(2)(E) to (J). added subpar. (E) and redesignated former subpars. (E) to (I) as (F) to (J), respectively.Pub. L. 114–190
amendments: Amendments2016—Subsec. (a)(2)(E) to (J). added subpar. (E) and redesignated former subpars. (E) to (I) as (F) to (J), respectively.Pub. L. 114–190

children.heading: Law enforcement terrorism prevention program
children.label: a Law enforcement terrorism prevention program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall ensure that not less than 25 percent of the total combined funds appropriated for grants under sections 604 and 605 of this title is used for law enforcement terrorism prevention activities.

children.label: A
children.text: information sharing and analysis;

children.label: B
children.text: target hardening;

children.label: C
children.text: threat recognition;

children.label: D
children.text: terrorist interdiction;

children.label: E
children.text: training exercises to enhance preparedness for and response to mass casualty and active shooter incidents and security events at public locations, including airports and mass transit systems;

children.label: F
children.text: overtime expenses consistent with a State homeland security plan, including for the provision of enhanced law enforcement operations in support of Federal agencies, including for increased border security and border crossing enforcement;

children.label: G
children.text: establishing, enhancing, and staffing with appropriately qualified personnel State, local, and regional fusion centers that comply with the guidelines established under ;section 124h(i) of this title

children.label: H
children.text: paying salaries and benefits for personnel, including individuals employed by the grant recipient on the date of the relevant grant application, to serve as qualified intelligence analysts;

children.label: I
children.text: any other activity permitted under the Fiscal Year 2007 Program Guidance of the Department for the Law Enforcement Terrorism Prevention Program; and

children.label: J
children.text: any other terrorism prevention activity authorized by the Administrator.

children.heading: Participation of underrepresented communities in fusion centers
children.label: 3 Participation of underrepresented communities in fusion centers
children.text: The Administrator shall ensure that grant funds described in paragraph (1) are used to support the participation, as appropriate, of law enforcement and other emergency response providers from rural and other underrepresented communities at risk from acts of terrorism in fusion centers.

children.heading: Office for State and Local Law Enforcement
children.label: b Office for State and Local Law Enforcement

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Policy Directorate of the Department an Office for State and Local Law Enforcement, which shall be headed by an Assistant Secretary for State and Local Law Enforcement.

children.heading: Qualifications
children.label: 2 Qualifications
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for State and Local Law Enforcement shall have an appropriate background with experience in law enforcement, intelligence, and other counterterrorism functions.

children.heading: Assignment of personnel
children.label: 3 Assignment of personnel
children.text: The Secretary shall assign to the Office for State and Local Law Enforcement permanent staff and, as appropriate and consistent with sections 316(c)(2), 381, and 468(d) of this title, other appropriate personnel detailed from other components of the Department to carry out the responsibilities under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: lead the coordination of Department-wide policies relating to the role of State and local law enforcement in preventing, preparing for, protecting against, and responding to natural disasters, acts of terrorism, and other man-made disasters within the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: serve as a liaison between State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies and the Department;

children.label: C
children.text: coordinate with the Office of Intelligence and Analysis to ensure the intelligence and information sharing requirements of State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies are being addressed;

children.label: D
children.text: work with the Administrator to ensure that law enforcement and terrorism-focused grants to State, local, and tribal government agencies, including grants under sections 604 and 605 of this title, the Commercial Equipment Direct Assistance Program, and other grants administered by the Department to support fusion centers and law enforcement-oriented programs, are appropriately focused on terrorism prevention activities;

children.label: E
children.text: coordinate with the Science and Technology Directorate, the Federal Emergency Management Agency, the Department of Justice, the National Institute of Justice, law enforcement organizations, and other appropriate entities to support the development, promulgation, and updating, as necessary, of national voluntary consensus standards for training and personal protective equipment to be used in a tactical environment by law enforcement officers; and

children.label: F
children.text: conduct, jointly with the Administrator, a study to determine the efficacy and feasibility of establishing specialized law enforcement deployment teams to assist State, local, and tribal governments in responding to natural disasters, acts of terrorism, or other man-made disasters and report on the results of that study to the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 5 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to diminish, supercede, or replace the responsibilities, authorities, or role of the Administrator.
